commit.message:
Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)
- pages/5: Stock Comparison (2 stocks, correlation, ratios)
- pages/6: Commodities Dashboard (gold, silver, oil, gas, copper, wheat)
- pages/7: Stock Analysis (any ticker, MAs, volatility, company info)

All charts rebuilt in Plotly. Consistent styling across all pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dashboard: only show active agents, auto-remove completed

Active agents are detected by output file mtime (60s threshold).
Completed agents are automatically removed from the dashboard.
Completed agents remain accessible via the agent browser (b key).
If no agents are active, the dashboard sits idle until one spawns.

commit.message:
Add agent browser, double-tap dismiss, and operator approval rule

Dashboard:
- Add agent browser (b key) — lists all agent transcripts from the
  session sorted by recency, with skill/agent-type labels extracted
  from JSONL content. Agents already in the dashboard shown with ●
  marker. Enter to add, Esc/b to close.
- Double-tap dismiss (d key) — first press shows "tap d again to
  dismiss" warning in yellow, second press within 2s actually removes
  the pane. Prevents accidental dismissals.
- Fix tasks directory path derivation in dashboard.sh — sed was
  stripping a leading dash that the actual directory keeps.
- Label extraction: detect Plan/Explore agents from prompt keywords,
  extract skill names from "Load skill" patterns, fall back to prompt
  summary instead of hex agent IDs.

Orchestrator:
- Add mandatory operator approval rule before every agent spawn.
  Presented as a top-level constraint alongside "don't run scanning
  tools". No agents launch without explicit operator sign-off.

fix(erofs): Fix inode field layout, wire fs-verity, unmount old composefs

- Fix compact inode: write i_mtime (u32) at offset 0x0C instead of zeros
- Fix extended inode: write i_nb (nlink u16) at offset 0x06 instead of zeros
- Wire up fs-verity enablement on CAS objects (was a TODO)
- Unmount old composefs mount before switching to new generation
- Move staging composefs mount to permanent /conary/mnt after switch
- Remove unused compression setting from generation builder

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Generate C function instead of static array for KASLR runtime use

Replace static uint64_t arrays with a void exploit_gen() function that
takes kern_base as a parameter, eliminating the need to recompile for
each KASLR base. Unaligned JOP dispatch entries use cast writes at the
exact byte offset instead of corrupting overlapping qword slots.

commit.message:
Credara Backend Implementation Plan (#1)

9-phase plan covering project setup, database schema, auth, school/class
management, lessons/content, assessments/submissions, assignments, progress
tracking, and badges.

Key decisions:
- TypeScript/Fastify + PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM
- Session-based auth scoped to user/school/role context
- user_school_roles join table for multi-school/role support
- Derived mastery scores (no materialized tables)
- GCP Cloud Run + Cloud SQL for hosting
- Docker for local dev and production builds

Also adds CLAUDE.md with project conventions and architecture overview.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Encrypt OAuth tokens at rest with per-org AES-256-GCM keys

- New src/crypto.js: AES-256-GCM encryption with keys derived from
  HMAC-SHA256(SESSION_SECRET, orgId) — each org gets a unique key
- All 5 OAuth refresh tokens (Drive, OneDrive, Box, Gmail, Outlook)
  encrypted before storage and decrypted on use
- Automatic migration: existing plaintext tokens encrypted on startup
- Backward compatible: gracefully handles missing SESSION_SECRET
- Updated architecture review response: token encryption implemented,
  self-hosting/single-tenant option documented
- Updated security spec to v1.2 with encryption details

fix: inject Authorization header directly from Cookie in proxy Route Handler

reliably to Route Handlers), read the httpOnly access_token cookie directly
and set the Authorization header before forwarding to FastAPI.

commit.message:
Refactor GenericSegmentTree: fix formulas, add validation, expand tests (#1251)

- Rewrite GenericSegmentTree with simplified RUF structure using 3 shared
  lambdas (addDelta, mulDelta, assignDelta) instead of 22 named fields
- Fix incorrect productQueryAssignUpdate formula (was returning d, now
  correctly returns d^count)
- Remove broken gcdQuerySumUpdate and productQuerySumUpdate RUFs
- Throw UnsupportedOperationException for GCD+ADDITION and PRODUCT+ADDITION
- Rename methods: rangeQuery1->rangeQuery, rangeUpdate1->rangeUpdate
- Delete GenericSegmentTree2 (Segment-object variant) and GenericSegmentTree3
  (commented-out pointer-based WIP) along with their tests and BUILD targets
- Expand test coverage from 14 to 38 tests including constructor validation,
  single-element arrays, edge cases, negative values, overlapping updates,
  and randomized tests for PRODUCT and MIN/MAX+MUL combos

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Algorithms
repository.description: A collection of algorithms and data structures

commit.message:
Fix SPA fallback: detect file extensions upfront instead of catching asset throws

The previous approach called env.ASSETS.fetch(request) and caught the throw
for non-existent paths, but the fallback to fetch("/") was unreliable.
Now we check for a file extension in the pathname first — static assets go
directly to ASSETS, everything else rewrites to "/" before hitting ASSETS.

feat: generic MCP server — serve any skill's tools via Model Context Protocol (#131)

Add a generic MCP server that dynamically loads any skill's tool schemas
and makes them callable over MCP. 13 skills with 41 tool definitions are
now immediately servable with stub handlers; real implementations can be
added incrementally via the handler plugin pattern (built-in > user > stub).

New modules:
- src/mcp/server.js — McpServer factory (createMcpServer + startServer)
- src/mcp/schema-converter.js — YAML tool params → Zod schemas
- src/mcp/handler-registry.js — priority-based handler resolution
- src/mcp/handlers/stub.js — structured "not implemented" responses
- bin/mcp-serve.js — standalone entry for npx @djm204/agent-skills-serve
- CLI: `agent-skills serve <skill>` subcommand with --handler-dir support

Integration:
- All 13 skills with tools updated to mcp_server: built-in
- Adapters (cursor, claude-code) emit agent-skills-serve for built-in MCP
- Public API exports createMcpServer() with TypeScript types
- YAML parser fixed to handle inline arrays [a, b, c]
- 61 new tests (433 → 494 total)

Fix rate limiting: use Haiku + reduce concurrency to 2

Sonnet 4.6 has 8K output TPM on free tier — 8 concurrent batches
instantly blows past it. Switch to Haiku (higher rate limits, faster,
cheaper) and drop CLAUDE_CONCURRENCY from 8 to 2. Matching quality
is unaffected — yes/no market comparison doesn't need Sonnet.

commit.message:
fix: change HTTPBearer to auto_error=False in deps/auth.py to return 401

auto_error=True causes FastAPI to return 403 when no Authorization header
is present. Changed to auto_error=False with explicit None check so that
unauthenticated requests return 401, consistent with dependencies/auth.py.

commit.message:
fix: install.bat adds CUDA detection and PyTorch installation

Modern pip (PEP 660) bypasses setup.py's custom cmdclass, so
install_pytorch_with_cuda() was never called. Now install.bat
explicitly detects CUDA version via nvidia-smi and installs
the correct PyTorch build (cu118/cu124) before pip install -e .

Also updates package-lock.json (react-is fix) and egg-info metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Credara Backend Implementation Plan

9-phase plan covering project setup, database schema, auth, school/class
management, lessons/content, assessments/submissions, assignments, progress
tracking, and badges.

Key decisions:
- TypeScript/Fastify + PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM
- Session-based auth scoped to user/school/role context
- user_school_roles join table for multi-school/role support
- Derived mastery scores (no materialized tables)
- GCP Cloud Run + Cloud SQL for hosting
- Docker for local dev and production builds

Also adds CLAUDE.md with project conventions and architecture overview.
